title: Groundtruth Eval
problem: A semantic layer for agents product has one bet the company question: when the agent answers, was the answer correct against the governed definition? Kaelio's marketing copy says "metrics defined in five places, governed in none" (kaelio.com) — but the only artifact a buyer sees today is a homepage and a list of integrations.
direction: An open benchmark + harness that scores any context layer grounded analytics agent on natural language -> governed metric correctness, designed to make Kaelio the reference standard.
why: I deliberately didn't pitch "build a new BI integration" (they have 900+) or "an analytics agent" (they have one) - instead I pitched the meta product that decides who wins the category. That's the judgment call.

archetypes.name: semantic evidence replay
archetypes.trigger: semantic signal changes while layer context is stale
archetypes.expected: block release until cited evidence is regenerated

archetypes.name: layer boundary probe
archetypes.trigger: layer handoff crosses a policy or trust boundary
archetypes.expected: route to reviewer with evidence packet

archetypes.name: agents regression harness
archetypes.trigger: agents behavior regresses against the last accepted fixture
archetypes.expected: open a regression issue with trace and benchmark delta

archetypes.name: product operator packet
archetypes.trigger: product output needs a human-readable audit packet
archetypes.expected: accept only if decision claims cite fixture evidence
